claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have?
- Their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. Alex is 8 years old, and Blake is 20. I'm trying to figure out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me help you assess eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Since Alex is 8 and Blake is 20, their situations will be different as there are age-related rules for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some important information:

**For Blake (who is 20):**

Is Blake currently in approved education or training? This could include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent)
- Approved training courses

This is important because there are different age limits depending on whether a young person is in education or training.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school a while back and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, that's important information.

Now let me ask about Alex (who is 8 years old):

**Does Alex live with you?** And are you the person primarily responsible for Alex's care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex does live with me, but their other parent also lives with us and already claims Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important information. That's a key factor - **only one person can get Child Benefit for the same child**. Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot also claim it for Alex, even though you live together and share care.

Let me now ask about Blake to complete the assessment:

**Does Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now make my assessment based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Results

Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake**. Here's why:

### For Alex (age 8):
❌ **Not eligible** - Although Alex is under 16 and lives with you, **only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child**. Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot also make a claim for them, even though you live together.

**Note:** If circumstances change and the other parent stops claiming, you could potentially claim instead. Only one person can claim at a time, so you and the other parent would need to decide between you who claims.

### For Blake (age 20):
❌ **Not eligible** - Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit:
- Stops at age 16 for young people not in education or training
- Can continue until age 20 **only if** the young person is in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training

Since Blake is not currently in education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for them.

---

If Blake were to start an approved education course or training program in the future, you might be able to claim then (as long as they're under 20 when they start). Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you, which would normally meet the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. Since someone else is already claiming for Alex, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit can only be claimed for young people aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is over the age limit for standard Child Benefit (which ends at age 16) and does not meet the extended criteria for 16-19 year olds (being in approved education or training), you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You have two children: Alex (8) and Blake (20). You are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. For Alex, although they meet the age requirement, their other parent already claims Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim per child. For Blake, they are over the standard age limit of 16 and do not qualify for the extended age provision (up to 20) because they are not currently in approved education or training.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. Since someone else is already claiming for Alex, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit can only be claimed for young people aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is over the age limit for standard Child Benefit (which ends at age 16) and does not meet the extended criteria for 16-19 year olds (being in approved education or training), you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: You have two children: Alex (8) and Blake (20). You are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. For Alex, although they meet the age requirement, their other parent already claims Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim per child. For Blake, they are over the standard age limit of 16 and do not qualify for the extended age provision (up to 20) because they are not currently in approved education or training.
